ogtitle: Authorise a payment with transaction signing
content: Skip to login   Skip to main content   Skip to chat     Business     Online banking     NAB Connect online banking     NAB Connect help     Payments     Payment processing and cut-off times     Authorise a payment with transaction signing   Authorise a payment with transaction signing   Learn how to authorise a payment with transaction signing in NAB Connect.    Why follow this guide?     To understand how to authorise a payment that met or exceeded the transaction signing limit.    Steps to authorise a payment with transaction signing   Make sure you’re logged into NAB Connect to get started.    Authorising with a mobile token    If you’re using a mobile token, you’ll see a QR code on your 'Payment authorisation' screen.     Launch the NAB Connect mobile app, select More and select Signing code.  Enter your four-digit PIN.  Scan the QR code from NAB Connect or enter the values displayed on screen manually.  A signing code will display on your mobile token. Type the code in the Enter signing code field at the bottom of the Authorise payments screen.  Select Submit. The payment has now been authorised.    Authorising with a physical token    If you’re a physical token user, follow the steps outlined to generate a one-time passcode to authorise payments.    When you select to Authorise a payment, a ‘Payment selected for Authorisation’ screen will appear. Note: This screen has additional instructions listed.     Press the Power button to turn on the physical token.  Enter the four-digit PIN and ‘SEL APP’ will display on the token screen.  Press the number 2. ‘1’ will display on the token screen.  Follow ‘Step 4’ from the on-screen instructions in NAB Connect and press the Power button.  ‘2’ will display on the token screen. Enter the number in Step 5 from the instructions on the screen in NAB Connect and press the Power button.  ‘3’ will display on the token screen. Enter the number in Step 6 from the instructions on the screen in NAB Connect and press the Power button.  Select Submit.    The payment has now been authorised.    Please note     A payment requiring transaction signing is usually one over $500,000. Depending on how your NAB Connect is configured, this final authoriser could be the same or a different person to the first authoriser.  Payments that require transaction signing cannot be bulk authorised.   You cannot authorise payments that require transaction signing codes using the authorise capability in the NAB Connect mobile app.    Related guides    See some of our other guides to help you get the most out of NAB Connect.    Daylight savings and public holiday payment processing   Check out our daylight savings and public holiday payment processing guide for NAB Connect.    Authorise a payment with a mobile or physical token   Use our guide to authorise a payment with a mobile or physical token in NAB Connect.    Create urgent domestic payments   Use our guide to make an urgent payment in NAB Connect.    Contact us for NAB Connect enquiries    Call us   Monday to Friday, 7:30am to 8:00pm (AEST/AEDT) Saturday, 9:00am to 2:00pm (AEST/AEDT)    Within Australia: 1300 888 413    Overseas: +61 3 8641 9930    Important information    This section contains Important Information relevant to the page you are viewing, but you can't see it because you have JavaScript disabled on your browser. Please enable JavaScript and come back so you can see the complete page. It's important that you read the Important Information in this section before acting on any information on this page.
